sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Advanced Biomed Signs Supplemental Agreement With Wei Ha Hui
feed.summary: Advanced Biomed (ADVB) has entered into a Supplemental Agreement with Wei Ha Hui concerning the spin-off of Advanced Biomed (HK). The agreement formalizes the repayment of $6,925,549 that Advanced Biomed (HK) owes the company, with the full amount to be repaid within one year. This amendment, approved by the board, adds the repayment obligation to the existing spin-off terms to ensure an orderly separation and recovery of intercompany receivables.

feed.title: Invesco Earnings Call Highlights Strategy Over Numbers
feed.summary: Invesco Ltd.'s recent Q4 earnings call emphasized strategic milestones and a client-centric transformation, signaling confidence in "accelerated profitable growth" for fiscal 2025. While the call was largely positive and forward-looking, it notably lacked specific quantitative financial details such as revenue, net income, or assets under management. The company directed investors to its website for more detailed financial information, leaving market participants with a qualitative but optimistic outlook on its future performance.

feed.title: Leidos (LDOS) Stock Sinks As Market Gains: Here's Why
feed.summary: Leidos (LDOS) stock experienced a 1.24% decline, closing at $187.15, while the broader S&P 500 gained 0.41%. Despite this daily dip, the company's shares have risen 2.16% over the past month, outperforming its sector and the S&P 500. Investors are anticipating Leidos's Q4 2025 earnings release on February 17, 2026, with analysts predicting an 8.44% EPS growth and a 2.58% revenue decrease year-over-year.

feed.title: ADM to pay $40M to settle SEC accounting fraud probe
feed.summary: Archer-Daniels-Midland Co. (ADM) has agreed to pay $40 million to settle SEC allegations that former executives manipulated the accounting of a struggling nutrition business unit to meet company targets. The company did not admit or deny the allegations, and the DOJ closed its investigation without further action. However, the SEC also filed a lawsuit against former CFO Vikram Luthar, accusing him of coordinating profit shifts to prop up the nutrition segment.

feed.title: PLDT plans P1.22- billion subscription to Kayana shares
feed.summary: PLDT Inc. announced its board has approved a P1.22-billion subscription to additional shares in Kayana Solutions, Inc., maintaining its 45% stake. This investment aims to create new value and growth opportunities, leveraging Kayana Solutions' role as a data-powered digital experience company. PLDT previously invested in Kayana in September last year, and its shares saw a slight increase on Tuesday.

feed.title: Runway Growth Finance Launches Unsecured Notes Offering
feed.summary: Runway Growth Finance (RWAY) has initiated an underwritten offering of unsecured notes to be listed on the Nasdaq Global Select Market. The net proceeds will primarily be used to repay existing debt, including $51.75 million of 8.00% notes due in December 2027, and to support the prior acquisition of SWK Holdings, which will boost its healthcare investment portfolio to 31%. Despite a 14% dividend yield and a 30% discount to NAV, analysts maintain a cautious outlook on RWAY's future performance, with an average 1-year price target of $10.57.

feed.title: State Street, Galaxy launch 3 new crypto ETFs
feed.summary: State Street Global Advisors and Galaxy Asset Management have launched three new cryptocurrency ETFs, aiming to provide diversified exposure to Web3 and blockchain technology. These actively managed funds, which charge fees ranging from 0.65% to 0.9%, invest in a mix of stocks and cryptocurrencies. The ETFs include the SPDR Galaxy Digital Asset Ecosystem ETF (DECO), the SPDR Galaxy Hedged Digital Asset Ecosystem ETF (HECO), and the SPDR Galaxy Transformative Tech Accelerators ETF (TEKX).

feed.title: Telecom software firm CSG picks up analyst wins in billing and roaming
feed.summary: CSG (NASDAQ: CSGS) has received multi-category recognition from leading analyst firms for its telecom software portfolio, including CPQ, monetization, and digital partner management. The company was named a Challenger in the 2026 Gartner Magic Quadrant for Configure, Price & Quote Application Suites, a Leader in MGI Research's 2025 Agile Billing 360 Ratings, and a High-Flyer in the Kaleido Intelligence Roaming Vendor Hub. These recognitions highlight CSG's role in helping communication service providers simplify complexity, monetize new services, and deliver trusted customer experiences.

feed.title: reAlpha (NASDAQ: AIRE) Expands Market Coverage Ahead of the Spring Homebuying Season
feed.summary: reAlpha Tech Corp. (Nasdaq: AIRE) has expanded its multi-state market coverage to 13 active states and Washington, D.C., through its recently acquired technology-enabled real estate brokerage, Prevu Inc. This expansion marks the first post-acquisition integration milestone, significantly increasing its ability to offer both real estate and mortgage brokerage services and commission rebates to homebuyers. The move positions reAlpha to provide buyer representation services in high-demand markets ahead of the spring homebuying season.

feed.title: Mondelēz International to Present at CAGNY Conference on February 17
feed.summary: Mondelēz International, Inc. announced that its Chairman & CEO, Dirk Van de Put, and EVP & CFO, Luca Zaramella, will present at the Consumer Analyst Group of New York (CAGNY) Conference on February 17 at 2:00 p.m. ET. A listen-only webcast and replay will be available on the company's website. The company reported approximately $36.4 billion in net revenues for 2024 and is known for brands like Oreo, Ritz, and Cadbury.

feed.title: NextEra Energy Shrugs Off Hyperscaler Competition Concerns
feed.summary: NextEra Energy Resources, the renewable energy branch of NextEra Energy, added approximately 13.5 gigawatts of new generation and battery storage projects to its backlog in 2025. The company appears unfazed by potential competition from hyperscalers in the energy sector. This indicates a strong growth trajectory for NextEra Energy in renewable energy development.

feed.title: Kroger services restored, delivery delays remain after winter storm
feed.summary: Following a recent winter storm, Kroger has successfully restored all pickup and delivery services and restocked shelves across the Tri-State area. However, major shipping companies like Amazon, USPS, FedEx, and UPS are still experiencing significant delivery delays. While weather-related delays typically do not qualify for automatic refunds, customers can try to file for money back after weather alerts are lifted, keeping in mind specific company timelines for such requests.

feed.title: Assessing Prologis (PLD) Valuation After Strong 2025 Revenue But Softer Earnings And 2026 Guidance
feed.summary: Prologis (PLD) recently reported strong 2025 revenue but softer net income and EPS, along with lower 2026 earnings guidance, causing a re-evaluation of its stock. Despite a narrative suggesting a 6.9% undervaluation at $138.25 due to robust rent growth potential, the stock's current P/E ratio of 36x is higher than both its peers and its own fair ratio. This suggests the market might be factoring in future growth, prompting investors to consider whether the premium is justified against potential risks like slower leasing and elevated bad debt.

feed.title: Insider Selling: Northern Trust (NASDAQ:NTRS) COO Sells $3,740,500.00 in Stock
feed.summary: Northern Trust (NASDAQ:NTRS) COO Peter Cherecwich sold 25,000 shares worth $3,740,500 on January 26th, reducing his stake by 48.77%. This insider sale comes shortly after the company reported stronger-than-expected Q4 earnings and several analysts raised their price targets, although the consensus remains a "Hold." The company also declared a quarterly dividend of $0.80 per share.

feed.title: Agilysys (AGYS) Margin Strength And EPS Growth Test High Valuation Narrative In Q3 2026
feed.summary: Agilysys (AGYS) recently released its Q3 2026 earnings, reporting robust revenue and EPS growth, with margins strengthening. While the company's financial performance highlights significant earnings growth over the past year and a strong five-year average, pushing its valuation to a premium compared to peers and industry averages, this growth could still support the higher P/E multiple. Investors are now assessing the durability of these margins and the consistency of its profit run rate against its current high valuation.

feed.title: More Than Two Dozen Groups Tell FCC to Reject Nexstar-Tegna Deal
feed.summary: Twenty-eight public-interest groups, labor unions, and civil-rights organizations have formally requested the FCC to deny the proposed merger between Nexstar and Tegna. They argue that the acquisition would create excessive concentration in local TV markets, increase cable and satellite prices, and harm local news and consumers, asserting that the FCC lacks the authority to approve such a deal. If approved, the merger would combine the nation's largest and fourth-largest television station conglomerates, resulting in a single entity operating 265 full-power stations across 44 states and 132 Designated Market Areas.

feed.title: Applied Optoelectronics (NASDAQ:AAOI) Shares Up 8.6% - What's Next?
feed.summary: Applied Optoelectronics (NASDAQ:AAOI) shares rose 8.6% on Tuesday, trading at $37.88 with below-average volume. Analysts are currently mixed on the stock, with a consensus "Hold" rating and an average price target of $35.60. Despite beating quarterly EPS expectations and reporting strong revenue growth, the company issued negative Q4 2025 EPS guidance, and insiders have recently sold shares.

feed.title: Deere to open two new U.S. facilities creating 300 jobs
feed.summary: John Deere announced plans to open two new U.S. facilities, a distribution center in Indiana and an excavator factory in North Carolina, which will create approximately 300 jobs. These investments are part of the company's strategy to expand manufacturing capabilities and improve supply chain efficiency, with a plan to invest $20 billion in U.S. manufacturing over the next decade. Despite trading near its 52-week high, some analysis suggests the stock may be overvalued.

feed.title: Equifax AI Fraud And Verification Push Meets Undervalued Share Price Outlook
feed.summary: Equifax has launched two new AI and machine learning tools, Synthetic Identity Risk and Income Confirm, to combat synthetic identity fraud and streamline income verification for lenders. Despite these new product rollouts addressing critical concerns in the lending industry, Equifax's stock has declined over the past year and is currently estimated by Simply Wall St to be trading 46.6% below its fair value. The company's strategy focuses on leveraging its data assets and AI capabilities, but investors are advised to monitor adoption rates and the company's high debt level.

feed.title: FICO (FICO) Set to Announce Q1 Earnings with Strong Growth Expectations
feed.summary: Fair Isaac Corp (FICO) is anticipated to report a significant year-over-year increase in EPS and revenue for Q1, with analysts projecting $7.08 EPS and $501.93 million in revenue. The company, known for its FICO credit scores and applied analytics, has a strong track record of exceeding estimates. Despite a high valuation indicated by its P/E and P/S ratios, FICO's financial health, robust growth, and positive analyst recommendations suggest a solid foundation, though investors should monitor liquidity, debt levels, and insider selling.

feed.title: PPG Industries sees soft European, industrial markets in 2026 after Q4 profit miss
feed.summary: PPG Industries expects continued soft demand in Europe and global industrial markets throughout 2026, particularly in the first half of the year, following a disappointing fourth-quarter profit that missed Wall Street expectations. The company attributes this to higher costs, an uncertain economic environment, and reduced demand from sectors like building and construction. Despite these challenges, PPG projects $50 million in savings from consolidating European manufacturing and reported an increase in total net sales for the fourth quarter.

feed.title: ZBH Alert: Hagens Berman Investigates Zimmer Biomet (ZBH) Over Alleged Emerging Market Failures and "Inconsistent" Execution
feed.summary: Hagens Berman is investigating Zimmer Biomet Holdings, Inc. (NYSE: ZBH) due to concerns that the company's senior management may have misled investors regarding the stability of its international business and 2025 revenue targets. Despite previous "high confidence" assurances, Zimmer Biomet later revealed distributor order cancellations in emerging markets and reduced its revenue guidance, leading to a 15% stock decline. The investigation focuses on whether the company violated federal securities laws by issuing overly optimistic guidance while allegedly concealing operational disruptions.

feed.title: Radware buys Pynt to bolster pre-production API security
feed.summary: Radware has acquired API security testing specialist Pynt to enhance its API security product line with pre-production testing capabilities. This acquisition aims to close the gap between shift-left and shift-right strategies, offering a unified platform for API discovery, posture management, business logic protection, runtime defense, and testing. Pynt's technology will continue to be available as a standalone offering while also being integrated into Radware's broader security portfolio.

feed.title: Analysts Offer Insights on Technology Companies: Vontier (VNT) and Twilio (TWLO)
feed.summary: UBS analyst Amit Mehrotra maintained a Buy rating on Vontier (VNT) with a $45.00 price target, highlighting a Moderate Buy consensus among analysts. Mizuho Securities analyst Siti Panigrahi reiterated a Buy rating on Twilio (TWLO) with a $150.00 price target, noting a general Moderate Buy consensus with potential for 15.7% upside. Both reports suggest a positive outlook for these technology companies in the eyes of financial analysts.

feed.title: CNB Financial Corp/PA (NASDAQ:CCNE) Beats Q4 2025 Earnings and Revenue Estimates
feed.summary: CNB Financial Corp/PA (NASDAQ:CCNE) reported strong Q4 2025 financial results, surpassing Wall Street's EPS and revenue estimates. The company's non-GAAP EPS was $0.88 against an $0.8031 consensus, and revenue reached $87.16 million, exceeding the $74.87 million estimate. This positive performance has led to a favorable market reaction, with the stock price increasing by approximately 1.03% in after-market trading.

feed.title: Target misses out on Fortune 'most admired' accolade for first time since 2001
feed.summary: Target has not been named in Fortune Magazine's World’s Most Admired Companies All-Star list for the first time in 24 years, falling out of the Top 50 for the 2026 list. This exclusion follows a tumultuous year for the Minneapolis retailer, marked by a 27% stock decline, boycotts from progressive groups due to scrapped diversity initiatives, and criticism for its silence during immigration crackdowns in Minnesota. Longtime CEO Brian Cornell also stepped down, replaced by COO Michael Fiddelke, amidst these controversies.

feed.title: Vistra completes $2.25 billion private offering of senior secured notes
feed.summary: Vistra Operations Company LLC, a subsidiary of Vistra Corp. (NYSE:VST), has successfully completed a private offering of $2.25 billion in senior secured notes to finance its acquisition of Cogentrix Energy and for general corporate purposes. The offering includes $1 billion in 4.700% notes due 2031 and $1.25 billion in 5.350% notes due 2036. These notes are secured by first-priority security interests and guaranteed by Vistra subsidiaries.

feed.title: Packaging Corp of America falls after quarterly results miss
feed.summary: Packaging Corp of America (PKG) experienced a stock decline after reporting its quarterly financial results, which missed analyst expectations. The news was released by Reuters via Refinitiv and is a brief report on the company's performance.

feed.title: Deere to Move Excavator Production From Japan to North Carolina
feed.summary: Deere & Co. plans to relocate its excavator production from Japan to a new $70 million facility in Kernersville, North Carolina, and establish a distribution center in Hebron, Indiana. This strategic move aims to strengthen American manufacturing, creating approximately 150 jobs at each new location. The company announced these plans in a statement on Tuesday.

feed.title: Healthcare: Managed Care Stocks Plunge Following Almost-Flat Medicare Rate Proposal
feed.summary: Managed care stocks experienced a significant downturn after the Trump administration proposed a near-flat Medicare Advantage rate increase for 2027, which was far below analyst expectations. Companies like UnitedHealth Group, Humana, and CVS Health saw their share prices plummet. This unexpected proposal contrasts sharply with the substantial 2026 rate increase and shifts the healthcare sector's focus from pharmaceutical pricing to managed care organizations.

feed.title: Northern Trust (NTRS) COO Cherecwich sells $3.74 million in stock
feed.summary: Northern Trust's Chief Operating Officer, Peter Cherecwich, sold 25,000 shares of company stock for $3.74 million on January 26, 2026. Following the sale, Cherecwich still holds significant direct and indirect ownership in NTRS. This transaction comes after the company reported strong fourth-quarter 2025 earnings which surpassed analyst expectations, leading to several price target increases from financial analysts.

feed.title: Mizuho upgrades Applied Materials stock to Outperform on WFE growth
feed.summary: Mizuho has upgraded Applied Materials (NASDAQ:AMAT) from Neutral to Outperform, raising its price target to $370 from $275, citing accelerating capital expenditure in the US, Taiwan, and Japan. The firm projects significant WFE growth for 2026 and 2027, driven by increased spending from TSMC and Intel. Applied Materials' strong financial health, impressive returns, and diversified revenue streams, particularly outside China, contributed to the bullish outlook.

feed.title: Anterix (NASDAQ:ATEX) Shares Up 15% - Should You Buy?
feed.summary: Anterix (NASDAQ:ATEX) saw its shares jump 15% on Tuesday on higher-than-average trading volume. The company has a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating from analysts with an average price target of $50, despite mixed individual ratings including an "overweight" from JPMorgan and a "strong-buy" from Zacks. Insider and institutional ownership is substantial, and the company recently beat quarterly EPS expectations.

feed.title: Trustmark Corp (NASDAQ:TRMK) Q4 2025 Earnings Beat Estimates Despite Revenue Miss
feed.summary: Trustmark Corporation reported Q4 2025 earnings per share of $0.97, surpassing analyst estimates of $0.93, despite revenue of $207.1 million falling short of the $210.2 million forecast. The company achieved record net income and total revenue for the full fiscal year 2025, with solid balance sheet growth, robust net interest margin, and an increased quarterly dividend. The market reaction to the mixed results was muted, with the stock showing little movement in after-hours trading.

feed.title: WATCH: How AI helps Republic Services keep workers safe, recycle efficiently
feed.summary: Republic Services has implemented AI technology in its Mesa recycling center to enhance efficiency and worker safety. Three AI robots have recovered 4.5 million tons of recyclables, reducing landfill waste and CO2 emissions. Additionally, AI on forklifts detects objects and people, stopping the vehicle to prevent accidents, showcasing the company's investment in advanced technology for both environmental and safety benefits.

feed.title: F5 shares jump after revenue and earnings beat in fiscal first quarter
feed.summary: F5 Inc. experienced a significant jump in shares after reporting strong revenue and earnings that surpassed analyst expectations in its fiscal first quarter. The application security firm reported adjusted EPS of $4.45 and revenue of $822 million, driven by strong systems revenue growth. The company also raised its full-year outlook, reflecting confidence in continued demand for its hybrid multicloud architectures and AI-driven solutions.

feed.title: PACCAR Financial, Parts rise despite softer truck demand
feed.summary: PACCAR reported solid financial results for 2025, with strong performance from its parts and financial services divisions mitigating a decline in truck deliveries and earnings. PACCAR Financial Services saw a 5% increase in revenue and an 11% rise in pretax income, while PACCAR Parts also achieved record annual revenue. The company expects improving freight fundamentals and continued investment in technology to support a stronger truck market in 2026.

feed.title: Packaging Corp of America (NYSE:PKG) Misses Q4 Estimates, Shares Drop on Weak Outlook
feed.summary: Packaging Corporation of America (NYSE:PKG) reported Q4 2025 financial results below Wall Street expectations for both revenue and EPS, leading to a share drop. The company's performance was impacted by its recent acquisition of Greif's containerboard business, including integration costs and operational challenges, alongside softer demand in its legacy business. Packaging Corp of America also provided a Q1 2026 outlook below current analyst consensus, leaving investors to focus on the company's future ability to realize acquisition benefits and meet full-year growth expectations.

feed.title: GE HealthCare Declares AI Essential: Radiology's Staffing Crisis Forces Workflow Revolution
feed.summary: GE HealthCare has declared artificial intelligence as essential for radiology departments, not a luxury, due to severe staffing shortages and increasing demand. AI tools are being utilized to optimize workflows, reduce clinician burnout, and boost capacity by leveraging previously unused healthcare data. The company is actively acquiring related technologies and forming partnerships to accelerate AI deployment across healthcare settings, addressing a critical workforce crisis in radiology.

feed.title: American Electric Power Recognized as Low-Risk Retirement Stock
feed.summary: American Electric Power (AEP) is highlighted as a low-risk retirement stock due to a recent price target increase by Barclays, strong investor interest from industry experts like Sean Russo, and a substantial $72 billion five-year capital investment plan. The company's attractive dividend yield of approximately 3.26% further enhances its appeal for stable returns. AEP's strategic focus on meeting increased power demand from data centers and manufacturing through its capital plan positions it for future growth and investor confidence.

feed.title: Packaging Corp Missed Estimates As Big Customers Ordered Less
feed.summary: Packaging Corp of America reported weaker-than-expected fourth-quarter results, missing both profit and sales forecasts due to softer demand for corrugated boxes from major consumer brands and higher operating costs. Management attributed the decreased orders to inflation and economic uncertainty, leading customers to keep inventory levels lean. The company also faced disruptions from a winter storm and anticipates significant charges and job cuts related to closing a Washington facility, highlighting the increasing importance of resilience for heavy-asset manufacturers.

feed.title: F5 Inc (NASDAQ:FFIV) Surpasses Q1 Estimates and Raises Full-Year Outlook, Sending Shares Higher
feed.summary: F5 Inc (NASDAQ:FFIV) reported strong first-quarter fiscal 2026 results, beating analyst expectations for both revenue and non-GAAP EPS. The company also significantly raised its full-year 2026 outlook, leading to a more than 10% jump in its stock during after-hours trading. Driven by growth in product revenue, particularly systems, and durable market demand drivers, F5 demonstrates confidence in its continued performance.

feed.title: American Electric Power (AEP) Draws Focus as Barclays Refreshes Utilities Models
feed.summary: Barclays has raised its price target for American Electric Power (AEP) to $121 from $117, maintaining an Equal Weight rating, as it refreshes valuations for utility companies ahead of fourth-quarter earnings. AEP is undertaking a significant grid modernization program and plans to invest $72 billion over five years to meet increasing demand driven by electrification and data centers, while also offering a dividend yield of approximately 3.26%. Despite AEP's potential, the article suggests that certain AI stocks may offer higher returns and lower downside risk.

feed.title: Totino’s Skips The Super Bowl To Expand Fan Universe Around Chazmo, The Loveable Alien
feed.summary: Totino's is forgoing a Super Bowl advertisement to instead launch a digital musical featuring its popular alien mascot, Chazmo. This strategic shift is driven by overwhelming fan engagement and online views exceeding 10 million for videos featuring the character. The brand aims to treat Chazmo as expandable intellectual property, creating longer-form entertainment to meet audience demand and compete in a market where brands are increasingly compared to entertainment experiences.

feed.title: Earnings call transcript: F5 Networks Q1 2026 beats earnings expectations
feed.summary: F5 Networks reported strong Q1 2026 earnings, surpassing EPS and revenue forecasts, which led to a 1.11% rise in its stock during aftermarket trading. The company's performance was driven by significant growth in systems revenue and increased demand from hybrid multi-cloud adoption, AI investments, and converged platforms. F5 Networks has also raised its revenue growth outlook for FY2026 and plans strategic investments for future growth.

feed.title: John Deere brings over 150 NC factory jobs, 150 more in Indiana
feed.summary: John Deere announced plans for two new U.S. facilities: a parts distribution center in Hebron, Indiana, and a $70 million excavator factory in Kernersville, North Carolina. These expansions are expected to create approximately 150 jobs at each location, with the North Carolina factory taking over excavator production from Japan. This move reinforces John Deere's commitment to investing $20 billion in U.S. manufacturing over the next decade.

feed.title: Delta Was Not Done After This Month’s Boeing Order — Now It’s Buying Airbus A330s And A350s Too
feed.summary: Delta Air Lines has announced a new order for 16 Airbus A330-900s and 15 Airbus A350-900s, with options for 20 more widebody aircraft. This follows a recent order for Boeing 787-10s, indicating Delta's continued expansion of its fleet. The new Airbus planes will help fill delivery gaps and provide greater range for long-haul routes, with deliveries starting in 2029.

feed.title: RTX CEO backs dividend after Trump attacks on weapons makers
feed.summary: The CEO of RTX, a major defense contractor, has affirmed the company's commitment to its dividend strategy despite recent political rhetoric. This statement comes after former President Donald Trump criticized defense manufacturers, an action that could raise concerns among investors regarding the stability of returns from such companies. By backing the dividend, RTX aims to reassure shareholders and maintain investor confidence in its financial performance.

feed.title: Churchill Capital Corp IX Reschedules Date of Extraordinary General Meeting
feed.summary: Churchill Capital Corp IX (CCIX) has rescheduled its extraordinary general meeting to vote on the business combination with Plus Automation, Inc. (PlusAI) from February 3, 2026, to February 11, 2026. This postponement allows Churchill IX more time to engage with shareholders, particularly after PlusAI's recent announcement regarding an expanded partnership with TRATON Group. The deadline for redemption requests has also been extended to February 9, 2026, and shareholders are encouraged to submit their proxies.

feed.title: Churchill Capital IX Delays Vote on Plus Automation Merger
feed.summary: Churchill Capital Corp. IX (CCIX) has postponed its shareholder vote on the proposed business combination with Plus Automation Inc. (PlusAI) from February 3 to February 11, 2026. This delay allows the SPAC more time to engage with shareholders, especially following PlusAI's expanded partnership with TRATON Group. The combined company intends to list its common stock and warrants on Nasdaq after the merger closes.
